page: Amazon Web Services m5n.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance type built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU performance and average memory bandwidth across both small and large block reads and writes. Single-core performance, however, registers as weak in stress-ng and gzip compression tests. Geekbench multi-core scores and ray tracing benchmarks also fall into the lower tier. Conversely, the instance demonstrates average capabilities in software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, alongside average performance for small-scale LLM inference tasks.

The m5n.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads, avoiding the cost overhead of local NVMe storage or specialized GPU hardware. However, the lack of local storage means all disk operations depend on EBS performance, and the weak single-core metrics make it less optimal for compute-heavy single-threaded tasks or larger LLM inference. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate multi-threaded applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, running on an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz under the Nitro hypervisor. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and providing an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show average multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput, though single-core execution and multi-threaded Geekbench scores are weak. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for balanced workloads that do not require local disk storage or high-end compute accelerators. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It offers average multi-core performance and 8 Gbps network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and average multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, DDR4 memory, and optimized network bandwidth without local storage overhead.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB DDR4 memory.
Nitro hypervisor architecture with 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Average performance in database operations, software compilation, and web serving.
Weak single-core CPU performance and weak multi-core Geekbench results.
EBS-only storage configuration with no local disk or GPU hardware.
Suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and database tasks.
